payload.source_text: The pocket money tracker system allows children to claim pocket money tasks as they are completed throughout the week.
Parents can detail tasks that should be completed and the monetary amount earned for that task.
As a task is completed it is linked to the child that completed it.
The children should be able to see the available tasks and the tasks they have completed that week.
At the end of each week the children can see the total amount they have earned that week.
The Parents can also see a report of how much each child has earned and the tasks they completed so that they can provide the pocket money earned to each child.
At the start of the next week all tasks become available again to be claimed.
Historical task data shall be available.
The resulting system shall be able to operate on iOS 16, Android 9 and Windows 11 or later.

payload.completeness_rule: This register SHALL contain the identifiers of ALL Source elements present in the ledger.

payload.evaluated_scope: All input material in this project

payload.completeness_rule: This register SHALL contain the identifiers of ALL ZachmanCell elements present in the ledger.

payload.description: Task - compensated activity unit with associated monetary value
payload.justification: Multiple signals reference tasks as strategic objects that are created, assigned, claimed, tracked, and compensated

payload.description: Earnings - monetary compensation accumulated through task completion
payload.justification: Signals reference earnings as strategic entity that is calculated, viewed, and distributed

payload.description: Task History - longitudinal record of task performance and outcomes
payload.justification: SG054 identifies historical task data as strategically important entity for analysis purposes

payload.description: Weekly pocket money cycle governing task claiming, completion tracking, and earnings calculation
payload.justification: Multiple signals reference weekly timeframe as strategic cycle for task and earnings management

payload.description: Child - task claimer and performer entitled to view tasks, track completion, and access earnings information
payload.justification: Multiple signals identify children as key stakeholder role with specific interaction patterns and entitlements

payload.description: Parent - task definer and compensation distributor requiring performance oversight capabilities
payload.justification: Signals establish parental role as strategic stakeholder responsible for system configuration and payment administration

payload.description: Enable children to claim and track earned pocket money through task completion on a weekly basis
payload.justification: SG049 expresses fundamental purpose aligning with enterprise goal - enabling children's interaction with the pocket money system through task claiming and tracking mechanisms

payload.description: Empower parents to establish monetized task assignments with defined compensation values
payload.justification: SG050 articulates parental motivation as a strategic goal - the ability to create and manage compensated tasks

payload.description: Provide children with comprehensive visibility into task inventory status and availability
payload.justification: SG051 captures purpose of transparency as strategic goal - ensuring children have access to task information

payload.description: Motivate children through access to weekly earnings performance summaries
payload.justification: SG052 identifies motivational goal of providing children visibility into their financial progress

payload.description: Support accurate compensation distribution through parental access to child performance data
payload.justification: SG053 establishes parental need for reporting as strategic goal - ensuring fairness and accuracy in payment

payload.description: Enable accountability and performance trend analysis through historical task data access
payload.justification: SG054 indicates stakeholder requirement for longitudinal analysis as strategic goal supporting oversight and improvement

payload.title: High Level Description
payload.description: Section: High Level Description

payload.completeness_rule: This register SHALL contain the identifiers of ALL Segment elements present in the ledger.

payload.description: Expresses the fundamental purpose of enabling children to claim and track completed pocket money tasks weekly.
Articulates parental motivation to define compensated task assignments with associated monetary values.
Captures the purpose of providing children visibility into available and completed task inventory.
Identifies the motivational goal of enabling children to view their weekly earnings summary.
Establishes parental need to access child performance reporting for accurate compensation distribution purposes.
Indicates stakeholder requirement for longitudinal task history access supporting accountability or trend analysis.
Section header indicating the commencement of conceptual system description.
Establishes the core business concept of children claiming completed tasks for monetary reward tracking.
Defines the parent capability to specify task entities with associated monetary value attributes.
Specifies the relationship between completed task entities and child entities through linkage.
Defines child actor visibility requirements for both available and completed task entities within temporal scope.
Establishes weekly aggregation capability for earned monetary totals visible to child actors.
Defines parent reporting capability displaying child-task-earnings relationships to support payment fulfillment.
Specifies the cyclical business rule for task availability reset at weekly temporal boundaries.
Mandates retention and accessibility of historical task data entities across temporal periods.
Logical association rule defining how task completion events are linked to child entities.
Data visibility requirements specifying logical access patterns for child users to task collections.
Temporal aggregation logic defining weekly computation and presentation of earned amounts to children.
Logical reporting interface providing parents aggregated view of child earnings and task completion data.
Periodic state reset logic defining weekly cycle behavior for task availability restoration.
Data retention requirement mandating logical persistence and accessibility of historical task records.
Platform compatibility constraint specifying concrete operating system versions for deployment.
Identifies children as operational actors who execute task claiming activities throughout the week on a temporal basis.
Identifies parents as operational actors responsible for task configuration and monetary value assignment activities.
Defines operational visibility requirements for child actors viewing available and completed tasks within weekly cycles.
Establishes weekly operational schedule boundary when child actors can access earned amount totals.
Defines parent operational role in accessing reports to execute pocket money distribution activities.
Specifies weekly operational cycle timing when task availability resets for claiming operations.
Defines deployment platform locations specifying where the system operates across iOS, Android, and Windows environments.

payload.completeness_rule: This register SHALL contain the identifiers of ALL Signal elements present in the ledger.
This register SHALL contain the identifiers of ALL Stakeholder elements present in the ledger.

payload.description: States historical data availability requirement but lacks operational retention schedule or access timing details.
